bridge_note.indication: Acute dyspnea and pleuritic chest pain; rule out pulmonary embolism
bridge_note.narrative: 64F POD#3 from total knee arthroplasty presenting with acute-onset dyspnea, pleuritic right-sided chest pain, and tachycardia to 118. D-dimer markedly elevated. Wells score 6. Evaluate for pulmonary embolism.

tech_sheet.description: CT pulmonary angiogram of the chest. Helical acquisition, 1.0 mm collimation, bolus-tracked on the main pulmonary artery.

pacs.prior_description: Comparison is made to the CT chest dated 2026-01-04, which demonstrated clear pulmonary arteries and no acute cardiopulmonary process.

radiology_model.findings:
Pulmonary arteries: Filling defect within the right lower lobe segmental and subsegmental pulmonary arteries consistent with acute pulmonary embolism. No saddle embolus.
Heart: RV/LV diameter ratio of 1.3 with straightening of the interventricular septum, indicating right heart strain. Contrast reflux into the IVC and hepatic veins.
Lungs: Small right pleural effusion. Peripheral wedge-shaped opacity in the right lower lobe compatible with pulmonary infarct. No pneumothorax.
Bones/soft tissues: No aggressive osseous lesion.
radiology_model.impression:
Acute right lower lobe segmental and subsegmental pulmonary embolism with CT signs of right heart strain.
Small right pleural effusion with probable pulmonary infarct.

radiologist_dictation: Agree with acute right lower lobe segmental and subsegmental pulmonary emboli. Emphasize the RV/LV ratio of 1.3 and septal straightening — these indicate right heart strain and should prompt urgent clinical risk stratification.
